899	  modules sending an open \(W\) to \(\mathcal{O}_X(W)\) when
900	  \(W \subset U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) and to \(0\) otherwise. This extension-by-zero
901	  presheaf is, canonically, the \emph{free presheaf of modules on the representable}
902	  \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_p})\): writing
903	  \(\mathbf{y}\,V = \operatorname{Hom}(-, V)\) for the Yoneda presheaf of the open
904	  \(V\) on the site of opens of \(X\), the free presheaf of modules
905	  \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,V)\) sends \(W\) to the free
906	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(W)\)-module on the set \(\operatorname{Hom}(W, V)\), which is
907	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(W)\) if \(W \subset V\) and \(0\) otherwise — exactly
908	  extension-by-zero of \(\mathcal{O}_X|_V\). Its defining universal property
909	  \[
910	    \operatorname{Hom}_{\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)}\bigl(
911	      \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,V),\, \mathcal{F}\bigr)
912	    \;\cong\; \mathcal{F}(V)
913	  \]
914	  is the free–forgetful adjunction for presheaves of modules composed with the
915	  Yoneda isomorphism \(\operatorname{Hom}(\mathbf{y}\,V, G) \cong G(V)\). The
916	  \emph{free presheaf complex} of \(\mathcal{U}\) is the chain complex
917	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) in the abelian category
918	  \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\) of presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules whose
919	  term in degree \(p\) is the direct sum
920	  \[
921	    K(\mathcal{U})_p
922	      = \bigoplus_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
923	        \operatorname{free}\bigl(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\bigr),
924	  \]
925	  with \(K(\mathcal{U})_p\) placed in degree \(p \geq 0\) (and \(0\) in negative
926	  degrees), and whose differential \(K(\mathcal{U})_{p+1} \to K(\mathcal{U})_p\) is
927	  the alternating sum \(\sum_{j=0}^{p+1} (-1)^j\) of the maps
928	  \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}) \to
929	  \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}})\)
930	  obtained by applying \(\operatorname{free}\) to the representable index-dropping
931	  maps \(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}} \to \mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots
932	  \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}}\) induced by the inclusions of opens
933	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}} \subset U_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}}\)
934	  that drop the index \(i_j\). Each summand is a \emph{projective} object of
935	  \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\), because the universal property above identifies
936	  \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_{i_0 \ldots
937	  i_p}), -)\) with the exact ``sections over \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\)'' functor
938	  \((-)(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p})\); this is the structural feature exploited below.
939	
940	  We assume the index set \(I\) is \emph{finite} (so the required coproducts exist in
941	  \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\); this matches the finiteness hypothesis of the
942	  comparison target). Here the direct sum \(\bigoplus\) is the categorical coproduct
943	  \(\coprod\) (the indexed \(\operatorname{Sigma}\) colimit), which for a finite index
944	  set coincides with the finite biproduct.
945	\end{definition}

1696	  % \begin{equation}
1697	  % \label{equation-extended}
1698	  % 0 \to
1699	  % M \to
1700	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to

1717	    \mathcal{C}^2 \;\to\; \cdots,

1723	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}, with augmentation the canonical map

1728	\begin{proof}

1731	  checked on the stalks, equivalently over each affine open of a basis. Restricting
1732	  to an affine open \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) on which \(\mathcal{F}|_U =
1733	  \widetilde{M}\) and over which the cover restricts to a standard cover \(U =
1734	  \bigcup D(f_i)\), the augmented {\v C}ech complex becomes the extended complex of
1735	  localisations
1736	  \(0 \to M \to \prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to \prod_{i_0 i_1} M_{f_{i_0} f_{i_1}} \to

1738	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}, witnessed by the prime-local contracting
1739	  homotopy \(h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}} i_0 \ldots i_p}\). As this
1740	  holds over every affine of the basis, the augmented complex is exact on \(X\).

1743	\begin{lemma}
1744	[Presheaf description of the higher direct images]
1745	  \label{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}
1746	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage_isSheafify_presheafCohomology}
1747	  \uses{def:higher_direct_image}
1748	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Tag 01XJ, Lemma
1749	  % \texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images} (the higher direct images are the
1750	  % sheafifications of the cohomology presheaves of preimages) (read from
1751	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L591--603).
1752	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to Y$ be a morphism of ringed spaces.
1753	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
1754	  % The sheaves $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ are the sheaves
1755	  % associated to the presheaves
1756	  % $$
1757	  % V \longmapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F})
1758	  % $$
1759	  % with restriction mappings as in Equation (\ref{equation-restriction-mapping}).
1760	  % There is a similar statement for $R^if_*$ applied to a
1761	  % bounded below complex $\mathcal{F}^\bullet$."
1762	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, Tag 01XJ
1763	  (\texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}).}
1764	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be a morphism of schemes and \(\mathcal{G}\) an
1765	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and assume the category \(X.\mathrm{Modules}\) of
1766	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules has injective resolutions
1767	  (\([\,\mathrm{HasInjectiveResolutions}\;X.\mathrm{Modules}\,]\)), so that the
1768	  derived pushforward \(R^k f_* = (f_*).\mathrm{rightDerived}\,k\) is defined (see the
1769	  NOTE on Definition~\ref{def:higher_direct_image}). For every \(k \geq 0\) the
1770	  higher direct image
1771	  \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G} = (f_*).\mathrm{rightDerived}\,k\,(\mathcal{G})\) is the sheaf
1772	  on \(S\) associated to the presheaf
1773	  \[
1774	    V \;\longmapsto\; H^k\bigl(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G}|_{f^{-1}(V)}\bigr),
1775	    \qquad V \subseteq S \text{ open},
1776	  \]
1777	  with the evident restriction maps. In particular \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G}\) vanishes
1778	  iff \(H^k(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G}|_{f^{-1}(V)}) = 0\) for every \(V\) in a basis of
1779	  the topology of \(S\); since the affine opens form such a basis, it suffices to
1780	  check the vanishing on affine \(V\). This is the local-on-the-base description
1781	  through which \(f_*\)-acyclicity is reduced to absolute cohomology vanishing on
1782	  preimages.
1783	\end{lemma}
1784	
1785	\begin{proof}
1786	  \uses{def:higher_direct_image}
1787	  Choose an injective resolution \(\mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in
1788	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. By definition \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G}\) is the \(k\)-th
1789	  cohomology sheaf of the complex \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\); since the abelian
1790	  structure on \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-modules computes cokernels and kernels objectwise on
1791	  the level of presheaves and then sheafifies, that cohomology sheaf is the
1792	  sheafification of the presheaf \(V \mapsto H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr)
1793	  = H^k\bigl(\mathcal{I}^\bullet(f^{-1}(V))\bigr) = H^k(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G}|_{f^{-1}(V)})\),
1794	  the last equality because \(\mathcal{I}^\bullet|_{f^{-1}(V)}\) is an injective (hence
1795	  acyclic) resolution computing the absolute cohomology of \(f^{-1}(V)\). Evaluation
1796	  of a sheafification vanishes exactly when the underlying presheaf vanishes on a basis,
1797	  giving the stated affine-local criterion.
1798	
1799	  The comparison identifying \((f_*).\mathrm{rightDerived}\,k\) with the
1800	  sheafification of the cohomology presheaf \(V \mapsto H^k(f^{-1}(V),
1801	  \mathcal{G}|_{f^{-1}(V)})\) is constructed directly from injective resolutions
1802	  in \(X.\mathrm{Modules}\): cohomology in \(S.\mathrm{Modules}\) is the
1803	  sheafification of the objectwise (presheaf-level) homology, because the
1804	  sheafification/inclusion adjunction preserves the finite limits and colimits
1805	  computing kernels and cokernels. No presheaf-level \(\delta\)-functor formalism
1806	  is required.
1807	\end{proof}

1809	\begin{lemma}
1810	[Affine open immersions are $(\cdot)_*$-acyclic and degenerate the composition]
1811	  \label{lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp}
1812	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage_openImmersion_comp}
1813	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}
1814	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
1815	  % \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing} (relative vanishing of higher direct
1816	  % images for affine morphisms) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
1817	  % L180--199).
1818	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.
1819	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
1820	  % If $f$ is affine then $R^if_*\mathcal{F} = 0$ for all $i > 0$."
1821	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
1822	  \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing}.}
1823	  Let \(X\) be separated, \(j : U \hookrightarrow X\) the open immersion of an affine
1824	  open \(U\), and \(f : X \to S\) any morphism, with composite \(g := f \circ j : U
1825	  \to S\). Then:
1826	  \begin{enumerate}
1827	    \item \(j\) is an affine morphism (an open immersion of an affine open into a
1828	      separated scheme), so its higher direct images vanish: \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H} = 0\)
1829	      for all \(q \geq 1\) and every quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-module
1830	      \(\mathcal{H}\); and
1831	    \item consequently, for every quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{H}\) on \(U\) and every
1832	      \(k \geq 0\),
1833	      \[
1834	        R^k f_*\bigl(j_*\mathcal{H}\bigr) \;\cong\; R^k g_*\mathcal{H}.
1835	      \]
1836	  \end{enumerate}
1837	\end{lemma}
1838	
1839	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "According to
1840	% Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}
1841	% the sheaf
1842	% $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
1843	% $V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)})$.
1844	% By assumption, whenever $V$ is affine we have that $f^{-1}(V)$ is
1845	% affine, see Morphisms, Definition \ref{morphisms-definition-affine}.
1846	% By Lemma \ref{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero} we conclude that
1847	% $H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)}) = 0$
1848	% whenever $V$ is affine. Since $S$ has a basis consisting of affine
1849	% opens we win." (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L187--198)
1850	\begin{proof}
1851	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf, lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived}
1852	  (1) An open immersion of an affine open \(U\) into a separated scheme \(X\) is an
1853	  affine morphism: separatedness makes the immersion \(j\) quasi-separated and the
1854	  preimage of any affine open of \(X\) meets \(U\) in an affine, so \(j\) is affine.
1855	  The relative affine vanishing for affine morphisms then gives \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H} =
1856	  0\) for \(q \geq 1\); concretely, by the presheaf description
1857	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}) \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H}\) is the
1858	  sheafification of \(V \mapsto H^q(j^{-1}(V), \mathcal{H})\), and for affine \(V\) the
1859	  preimage \(j^{-1}(V)\) is affine, on which the Serre vanishing of
1860	  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing} kills \(H^q\) for \(q \geq 1\).
1861	
1862	  (2) Choose an injective resolution \(\mathcal{H} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in
1863	  \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-modules and apply \(j_*\) degreewise. Since each \(\mathcal{I}^n\)
1864	  is \(j_*\)-acyclic by part (1) (\(R^q j_*\mathcal{I}^n = 0\) for \(q \geq 1\)), the
1865	  complex \(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) is a resolution of \(j_*\mathcal{H}\).
1866	
1867	  Each \(j_*\mathcal{I}^n\) is \(f_*\)-acyclic. By the presheaf description
1868	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}), \(R^k f_*(j_*\mathcal{I}^n)\) is the
1869	  sheafification of
1870	  \[
1871	    V \;\longmapsto\; H^k\bigl(f^{-1}(V), (j_*\mathcal{I}^n)|_{f^{-1}(V)}\bigr)
1872	      \;=\; H^k\bigl(U \cap f^{-1}(V),\, \mathcal{I}^n|_{U \cap f^{-1}(V)}\bigr),
1873	  \]
1874	  the equality because \((j_*\mathcal{I}^n)|_{f^{-1}(V)}\) is the pushforward of
1875	  \(\mathcal{I}^n|_{U \cap f^{-1}(V)}\) along the open immersion
1876	  \(U \cap f^{-1}(V) \hookrightarrow f^{-1}(V)\), which leaves cohomology unchanged. For
1877	  \(V = \operatorname{Spec}(A) \subseteq S\) affine, \(f^{-1}(V)\) is quasi-compact and,
1878	  \(X\) being separated and \(j\) affine, the affine open \(U\) meets it in the affine
1879	  open \(U \cap f^{-1}(V)\); hence by Serre vanishing
1880	  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing} the cohomology
1881	  \(H^k(U \cap f^{-1}(V),\, \mathcal{I}^n|_{U \cap f^{-1}(V)}) = 0\) for \(k \geq 1\).
1882	  Since the affine opens form a basis of \(S\), this forces
1883	  \(R^k f_*(j_*\mathcal{I}^n) = 0\) for all \(k \geq 1\).
1884	
1885	  Thus \(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) is an \(f_*\)-acyclic resolution of
1886	  \(j_*\mathcal{H}\), and the acyclic-resolution comparison
1887	  Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived} applied with \(G = f_*\) gives,
1888	  for every \(k\),
1889	  \[
1890	    R^k f_*\bigl(j_*\mathcal{H}\bigr)
1891	      \;\cong\; H^k\bigl(f_*(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)\bigr)
1892	      \;=\; H^k\bigl((f \circ j)_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\bigr)
1893	      \;=\; R^k g_*\mathcal{H},
1894	  \]
1895	  the middle equality because \(f_* \circ j_* = (f \circ j)_* = g_*\), and the last
1896	  because \(\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) is an injective resolution of \(\mathcal{H}\)
1897	  computing \(R^k g_*\mathcal{H}\).
1898	\end{proof}

1928	% the sheaf
1929	% $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
1930	% $V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)})$.
1931	% By assumption, whenever $V$ is affine we have that $f^{-1}(V)$ is
1932	% affine, see Morphisms, Definition \ref{morphisms-definition-affine}.

1939	  A product of acyclic objects is acyclic, so it suffices to treat a single factor
1940	  \((j_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s})\), where \(s = (i_0, \ldots, i_p)\), \(U_s =
1941	  U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is affine, and \(j_s : U_s \hookrightarrow X\) is the open
1942	  immersion. Acyclicity for \(f_*\) is a local question on \(S\): the derived
1943	  pushforward \(R^k f_*\, \mathcal{G}\) is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
1944	  \(V \mapsto H^k(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G}|_{f^{-1}(V)})\), so it vanishes for
1945	  \(k \geq 1\) iff that cohomology vanishes for all affine \(V \subseteq S\).
1946	  Restricting to such a \(V = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\), the base change \(f_V :
1947	  f^{-1}(V) \to V\) is again separated and quasi-compact over the separated base
1948	  \(V\), hence \(f^{-1}(V)\) is separated and the affine \(U_s\) meets it in the
1949	  affine open \(U_s \cap f^{-1}(V)\).
1950	
1951	  Now decompose the structure map \(g_s := f \circ j_s : U_s \to S\). First,
1952	  \(j_s\) is the inclusion of an affine open into the separated scheme \(X\), hence
1953	  an affine morphism, so the higher direct images \(R^q (j_s)_*\) vanish for
1954	  \(q \geq 1\). By Lemma~\ref{lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp} part (2),
1955	  \(R^k f_*\bigl((j_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s})\bigr) \cong
1956	  R^k (g_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s})\). Second, working locally over an affine
1957	  \(V \subseteq S\) as above, \(g_s\) restricts to a morphism of affine schemes
1958	  \(U_s \cap f^{-1}(V) \to V\), which is an affine morphism; by the relative affine
1959	  vanishing \(R^k (g_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s}) = 0\) for \(k \geq 1\). That relative
1960	  vanishing is itself a consequence of Serre vanishing
1961	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing}): \(R^k(g_s)_*\mathcal{G}\) is the
1962	  sheafification of \(V \mapsto H^k(g_s^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G})\), and for affine
1963	  \(V\) the preimage \(g_s^{-1}(V)\) is affine, on which higher cohomology of a
1964	  quasi-coherent sheaf vanishes. Combining, \(R^k f_*(\mathcal{C}^p) = 0\) for all
1965	  \(k \geq 1\).
